title: Basic hand cream
subtitle: A simple oil-in-water emulsion with a broad-spectrum preservative
excerpt: A basic hand cream made as an oil-in-water emulsion with emulsifying wax, sweet almond oil, and shea butter. Water-containing; requires a broad-spectrum preservative. The recipe produces 200 g of a light, non-greasy hand cream that absorbs quickly.
sourceNotes: Point of Interest (swiftcraftymonkey.blogspot.com) lotion formulation guides. Bramble Berry emulsifying wax notes (brambleberry.com, May 2026). Optiphen Plus preservative usage guidelines (Bramble Berry, accessed May 2026).

content.text: A basic hand cream. Oil phase: sweet almond oil, shea butter, and emulsifying wax. Water phase: distilled water. The
phase-blend
technique combines both phases at 70-75°C with a stick blender to form a stable emulsion. A
broad-spectrum preservative
added at the cool-down phase extends shelf life to 3 months. Without a preservative this cream grows bacteria and mould within days.

This recipe uses Optiphen Plus (Phenoxyethanol and Caprylyl Glycol) at 1% of the total batch weight, added when the cream has cooled below 40°C. Other effective options: Geogard ECT at 1%, or Liquid Germall Plus at 0.5%. All three are available from cosmetic-ingredient suppliers. Do not substitute food-preserving vinegar or citric acid: they are not effective against cosmetic pathogens at safe-to-use skin concentrations.
What you need

items.name: Sweet almond oil
items.prepNote: sweet almond oil. 10% of the batch. Light carrier that absorbs without a heavy residue.

items.name: Shea butter
items.prepNote: refined shea butter. 5% of the batch. Conditioning and slightly occlusive.

items.name: Beeswax pellets
items.prepNote: emulsifying wax (not plain beeswax). INCI: Cetearyl Alcohol and Polysorbate 60. 7% of the batch. The emulsifier that makes this a stable lotion rather than separated oil and water.

items.name: Distilled water
items.prepNote: distilled or deionised water. 76% of the batch. Tap water is not suitable; minerals interfere with emulsification and introduce contamination.

items.name: Broad-spectrum preservative (Optiphen Plus)
items.prepNote: broad-spectrum cosmetic preservative (Optiphen Plus or equivalent). 1% of the total batch weight (2 g). Added to the cool-down phase below 40°C. Options: Optiphen Plus at 1%, Geogard ECT at 1%, or Liquid Germall Plus at 0.5%.

content.text: Method
Heat both phases to 70-75°C separately

Place the sweet almond oil, shea butter, and emulsifying wax in one heatproof bowl over simmering water. In a second heatproof bowl or a measuring jug, heat the distilled water over simmering water. Heat both phases to 70-75°C simultaneously, checking with the probe thermometer. Both phases must reach the same temperature; a significant temperature difference between phases prevents stable emulsification.
Combine and emulsify

When both phases are at 70-75°C, pour the water phase slowly into the oil phase in a steady stream while blending with the stick blender. Keep the blender head below the surface. The mixture will turn from translucent liquid to an opaque, white cream as the
emulsification
proceeds; this takes 1-2 minutes of blending. Continue blending until the cream is uniform and no oil droplets are visible on the surface.
Cool and add the preservative

Allow the cream to cool, stirring occasionally to prevent a skin forming on the surface. When the probe reads below 40°C, add the preservative (Optiphen Plus at 1% of the total batch weight) and stir thoroughly to incorporate. The cream is now at the cool-down phase. Continue stirring as it cools to room temperature.
Bottle the cream

Pour or spoon the cooled cream into clean, dry pump-dispenser bottles. Label with the batch date. Allow to set fully for 24 hours before assessing the final texture; lotions continue to thicken slightly after bottling.
Patch test

Apply a small amount to the inside of the wrist and wait 24 to 48 hours before regular use. Emulsifying wax contains cetearyl alcohol, a fatty alcohol that can sometimes cause contact dermatitis in sensitive skin.
Storage and shelf life

3 months at room temperature with a broad-spectrum preservative at 1%. Refrigerate if you want to extend to 6 months. Discard if the cream smells sour, shows any colour change, shows visible separation (water and oil pulling apart), or shows any visible mould growth on the surface or at the pump head.
